authors: Kapural L, Yu C, Doust MW, Gliner BE, Vallejo R, Sitzman BT, Amirdelfan K, Morgan DM, Yearwood TL, Bundschu R, Yang T, Benyamin R, Burgher AH
title: Comparison of 10-kHz High-Frequency and Traditional Low-Frequency Spinal Cord Stimulation for the Treatment of Chronic Back and Leg Pain: 24-Month Results From a Multicenter, Randomized, Controlled Pivotal Trial
study_question: To compare long-term results of HF10 therapy and traditional low-frequency SCS for chronic back and leg pain.
population_assessed: Patients with chronic intractable back and leg pain (VAS ≥5.0/10.0 cm) refractory to conservative therapy.
outcome_measures: Responder rate (≥50% back pain reduction), leg pain responder rate, percent pain relief, disability level.
inclusion_criteria: Chronic intractable back/leg pain (VAS ≥5.0), refractory to conservative therapy, ODI 41-80, surgical candidate.
answer_to_study_question: HF10 therapy demonstrated long-term superiority over traditional SCS in treating both chronic back and leg pain.
